privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.subtitle: Progamer.info is a personally operated game data analysis and utility website.

notice.title: Unofficial Fan Website Notice
notice.body: This website is not an official Top War website. It is an unofficial fan utility website operated personally.

summary.title: Summary
items: This website does not provide a membership registration feature.
This website does not directly collect personal information such as names, addresses, or phone numbers.
Cookies may be used for analytics and advertising.
Privacy practices on external websites are governed by the policies of those websites.

sections.title: 1. General Information
sections.body: Progamer.info values user privacy. This website does not provide a membership registration feature and does not directly collect personal information such as names, addresses, or phone numbers.

sections.title: 2. Analytics
sections.body: This website may use third-party analytics tools such as Google Analytics to improve the service and analyze usage. During this process, information such as visited pages, device environment, browser information, and approximate location may be collected through cookies or similar technologies.

sections.title: 3. Advertising and Cookies
sections.body: This website may use third-party advertising services such as Google AdSense. Third-party vendors, including Google, may use cookies to serve personalized ads based on a user's prior visits.

sections.title: 4. Cookie Management
sections.body: Users can refuse cookie storage or delete existing cookies through their browser settings. However, blocking cookies may cause some features or advertisements to not function properly.

sections.title: 5. External Links
sections.body: This website may contain links to external websites such as Discord, KakaoTalk, GitHub, YouTube, or other services. Privacy practices on external websites are governed by the policies of those websites.

sections.title: 6. Game-related Data
sections.body: Game-related calculations, rankings, simulations, and information provided on this website are for reference only. Game names, images, data, trademarks, and related materials belong to their respective rights holders.

sections.title: 7. Contact
sections.body: For inquiries related to this Privacy Policy or the use of this website, please contact us through the contact page.

contact.title: Contact
contact.subtitle: You can contact us by email for site errors, data correction requests, or feature suggestions.

notice.title: Please read before contacting
notice.body: Progamer.info is an unofficial fan utility website operated personally. For game accounts, payments, or official support issues, please contact the game operator directly.

methods.title: Email
methods.description: For site operation, privacy, advertising, and error reports

notes.title: Helpful information to include
items: The page URL where the issue occurred
Your selected language and device environment
The situation or input values that caused the issue
Screenshots or steps to reproduce the issue
For data correction requests, the related server number and reference date

footer.title: Operation Notice
footer.body: This website is operated as a personal project, and responses may not be immediate. However, reports about site errors, incorrect data, or usability issues will be reviewed when possible.

disclaimer.title: Disclaimer
disclaimer.subtitle: This page explains the scope and limitations of the information and features provided by Progamer.info.

notice.title: Unofficial Fan Website Notice
notice.body: Progamer.info is not an official Top War website and is operated personally without direct affiliation with the game operator.

summary.title: Summary
items: This website is not an official Top War website.
Calculations, rankings, and simulation results are provided for reference only.
Game names, images, data, trademarks, and related materials belong to their respective rights holders.
Users are responsible for their own decisions and actions based on information from this website.

sections.title: 1. Unofficial Website Notice
sections.body: Progamer.info is an unofficial fan utility website operated personally. This website is not an official Top War website and is not affiliated with, sponsored by, or approved by the game operator or related companies.

sections.title: 2. Game-related Information
sections.body: Calculators, simulators, rankings, guides, and data analysis materials provided on this website are intended as reference information to support gameplay. Actual in-game values, results, policies, and event details may change according to updates by the game operator.

sections.title: 3. Accuracy of Information
sections.body: Progamer.info strives to provide accurate information, but does not guarantee the completeness, accuracy, or timeliness of all information. Calculation or simulation results may differ from actual in-game results.

sections.title: 4. Ownership of Rights
sections.body: Game names, images, icons, data, trademarks, and other related materials, including those related to Top War, belong to their respective rights holders. This website does not officially own or represent those materials.

sections.title: 5. User Responsibility
sections.body: In-game decisions, purchases, account management, server transfers, event participation, and other actions based on information from this website are made at the user's own discretion and responsibility. This website is not responsible for any loss or disadvantage resulting from such actions.

footer.title: Contact and Correction Requests
footer.body: If you find incorrect information, have concerns about rights infringement, or want to request a data correction, please contact us through the contact page. We will review and respond within a reasonable scope.

header.title: Special Package Shop Calculator
header.description: Select the skill shard packages you plan to purchase and calculate the total payment, final shard amount, and effective cost per shard after applying double coupons.

package.title: Skill Shard Packages
package.description: Selecting the final package tier automatically selects all preceding tiers.

summary.title: Purchase Summary
summary.emptyDescription: Select a package to display the calculation results.
summary.discountMessage: Double coupons reduced the effective unit cost by {{percent}}%.
summary.bonusMessage: You receive {{count}} additional skill shards for the same payment.

guide.introductionTitle: What Is the Special Package Shop Calculator?
guide.introduction1: This tool calculates the total purchase price and number of skill shards obtained from Special Package Shop offers. When you select a package tier, all preceding tiers are included automatically. You can also apply a double coupon to individual packages and compare the resulting cost per shard.
guide.introduction2: Higher-tier packages may appear more expensive when comparing only their prices. Because each package provides a different number of shards, the cost per shard is a more useful measure of actual value.
guide.usage1: Select the purchase checkbox for the highest package tier you plan to buy.
guide.usage2: All preceding package tiers are automatically included.
guide.usage3: Select the double-coupon checkbox for each package where a coupon will be used.
guide.usage4: Review the total payment, final shard amount, and average cost per shard in the summary.
guide.exampleDescription: The tier 1 package provides 40 skill shards for 7,000 KRW, resulting in a base cost of 175 KRW per shard. With a double coupon, the same payment provides 80 shards, reducing the cost to 87.5 KRW per shard.
guide.caution1: This calculator assumes that packages must be purchased sequentially from the lowest tier.
guide.caution2: A double coupon does not reduce the package price. It doubles the number of skill shards received.
guide.caution3: Actual prices may vary depending on the device, app store, country, exchange rate, or game policy.
guide.caution4: The results may differ if package prices or shard amounts are changed in a future game update.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.question1: Can I purchase only a package from the middle of the sequence?
faq.answer1: This calculator follows a sequential purchase system in which earlier tiers must be purchased before later tiers are unlocked. Selecting a tier automatically includes all preceding tiers.
faq.question2: Does using a double coupon also double the price?
faq.answer2: No. The payment remains the same, while only the number of skill shards received is doubled.
faq.question3: Which package is the best one for a double coupon?
faq.answer3: A double coupon provides a larger bonus when used on a package with more base shards. Coupon eligibility and the number of coupons available should also be considered.

aria.doubleCoupon: Apply a double coupon to tier {{stage}} package

header.title: Exclusive Skill Calculator
header.description: Calculate the exclusive skill shards required for your target level and include your currently owned shards and lower-level exclusive skills to see the remaining shortage.

price.label: 1. Price of a Level 1 Exclusive Skill
price.description: Select the number of exclusive skill shards required to purchase the hero's Level 1 exclusive skill.

target.label: 2. Target Exclusive Skill Level
target.description: Select the final exclusive skill level you want to create.
target.required: You need {{count}} shards to reach the target level.

ownedShard.title: 3. Exclusive Skill Shards You Own
ownedShard.toggle: I currently have exclusive skill shards.

ownedSkill.title: 4. Exclusive Skills You Own
ownedSkill.toggle: I already have lower-level exclusive skills.
ownedSkill.noLowerLevel: There are no lower-level exclusive skills to enter because the target is Level 1.
ownedSkill.convertedTotal: Your owned exclusive skills are worth {{count}} shards.

result.title: Calculation Result
result.successTitle: You can create the target level.
result.successRemaining: After crafting, {{count}} shards of value will remain.
result.shortageTitle: You need more exclusive skill shards.

guide.introductionTitle: What Is the Exclusive Skill Calculator?
guide.introduction1: This calculator shows the total number of exclusive skill shards required to create a target exclusive skill level. It includes both loose shards and lower-level exclusive skills by converting them into their shard value.
guide.introduction2: The base purchase price differs by hero, so select the correct hero price before checking the result.
guide.usage1: Select the exclusive skill purchase price for the hero.
guide.usage2: Select the exclusive skill level you want to create.
guide.usage3: Enter the number of loose exclusive skill shards you currently own.
guide.usage4: Enter the number of lower-level exclusive skills you already own for each level.
guide.usage5: Check the additional shards required or the amount remaining after crafting.
guide.calculationDescription: The required value triples each time the exclusive skill level increases by one.
guide.targetFormulaTitle: Shards Required for the Target Level
guide.exampleDescription: The following example creates a Level 7 exclusive skill with a base price of 25 shards.
guide.caution1: Select the correct exclusive skill purchase price for the hero.
guide.caution2: The actual result may differ if crafting rules or prices change after a game update.
guide.caution3: Only skills below the selected target level can be entered as owned skills.
guide.caution4: The result is based on shard value and does not include any other in-game crafting requirements.

example.ownedLevel5: Value of one owned Level 5 skill

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.question1: Are owned exclusive skills converted into shards?
faq.answer1: Yes. Each owned exclusive skill is converted into the shard value required to craft that level and included in your total owned value.
faq.question2: Why are the purchase prices different?
faq.answer2: The base price of a Level 1 exclusive skill differs by hero. Check the hero's price before calculating.
faq.question3: What should I do if the result differs from the game?
faq.answer3: First verify the selected purchase price and owned quantities. The calculation may differ if the game's crafting rules or prices have changed.

header.title: Vitality Recovery Speed Calculator
header.description: Calculate your current vitality recovery interval and compare how much a new decoration reduces the recovery time.

current.title: Enter Current Base Information
current.description: Open the Base Information screen in the game and enter the displayed Vitality Recovery Speed and Base Vitality Recovery Speed.
current.imageAlt: Location of vitality recovery speed values on the Base Information screen
current.resultDescription: The estimated interval required to recover 1 vitality with the current settings.

decoration.title: Enter Decoration Information
decoration.description: Enter the Base Vitality Recovery Speed bonus of the decoration you plan to purchase or place.
decoration.imageAlt: Location of the base vitality recovery speed bonus on the decoration information screen
decoration.addSpeedLabel: Decoration's Base Vitality Recovery Speed Bonus
decoration.resultLabel: Time to recover 1 vitality after applying the decoration
decoration.resultDescription: The estimated interval required to recover 1 vitality after applying the entered decoration.

comparison.title: Effect Comparison
comparison.theoreticalNotice: Hourly and daily recovery values are theoretical estimates that assume vitality is continuously consumed and recovery never stops.

validation.invalid: Enter a valid number of 0 or greater in every field.

guide.introductionTitle: What Is the Vitality Recovery Speed Calculator?
guide.introduction1: This tool calculates the actual time required to recover 1 vitality using the Vitality Recovery Speed and Base Vitality Recovery Speed shown in Base Information.
guide.introduction2: Enter a decoration's Base Vitality Recovery Speed bonus to compare the recovery interval, time reduction, improvement percentage, and estimated recovery amount before and after applying it.
guide.usage1: Open Base Information in the game and locate Vitality Recovery Speed and Base Vitality Recovery Speed.
guide.usage2: Enter both values exactly as displayed in the first input section.
guide.usage3: Enter the Base Vitality Recovery Speed bonus of the decoration you plan to purchase or place.
guide.usage4: Review the recovery time and estimated hourly and daily recovery before and after applying the decoration.
guide.formulaDescription: The calculation starts from a base recovery time of 360 seconds and applies the relationship between total recovery speed and base recovery speed.
guide.currentFormula: ((100 - Vitality Recovery Speed + Base Vitality Recovery Speed) ÷ (100 + Base Vitality Recovery Speed)) × 360 seconds
guide.futureFormula: ((100 - (Vitality Recovery Speed + Decoration Bonus) + (Base Vitality Recovery Speed + Decoration Bonus)) ÷ (100 + Base Vitality Recovery Speed + Decoration Bonus)) × 360 seconds
guide.caution1: Enter the percentage values exactly as displayed in the game.
guide.caution2: For better accuracy, do not round values that include decimal places.
guide.caution3: Hourly and daily recovery amounts assume that vitality never reaches its maximum capacity.
guide.caution4: Actual results may differ if the recovery formula changes in a future game update.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.question1: What is the difference between Vitality Recovery Speed and Base Vitality Recovery Speed?
faq.answer1: Vitality Recovery Speed includes the currently applied total bonuses, while Base Vitality Recovery Speed is a separate value used by the formula. Both values are required for an accurate result.
faq.question2: Which decoration value should I enter?
faq.answer2: Enter the Base Vitality Recovery Speed percentage shown in the decoration details. Do not confuse it with a general Vitality Recovery Speed bonus.
faq.question3: Why should I enter decimal values?
faq.answer3: Even a small difference in the percentage can change the recovery interval by several seconds. Enter the displayed decimal value whenever available.

header.title: Lucky Box
header.description: Open a box to randomly reveal 1 to 3 unique rewards from a set of 28 reward images.

game.title: Open the Lucky Box
game.description: Click the box image or the open button to determine the rewards for this draw.
game.closedBoxAlt: Closed Lucky Box before drawing rewards
game.openBoxAlt: Opened Lucky Box after the reward draw

result.empty: Your rewards will appear here after opening the box.
result.ready: Click the box to start the draw.

guide.introductionTitle: What Is the Lucky Box Simulator?
guide.introduction1: This random reward tool recreates the feeling of opening a Lucky Box during a game event.
guide.introduction2: Rewards do not repeat within the same draw. After viewing the result, you can reset the box and draw again.
guide.usage1: Click the closed box image or the Open Lucky Box button.
guide.usage2: After the short opening animation, the selected reward images will appear.
guide.usage3: Click Draw Again to reset the box and generate a new result.
guide.rule1: Each draw selects between {{min}} and {{max}} rewards.
guide.rule2: Reward numbers range from 1 to {{limit}}, with no duplicates in the same draw.
guide.rule3: When supported by the browser, random numbers are generated with the Web Crypto API.
guide.notice: This page is an entertainment-only simulator. It does not award real items or monetary value and does not guarantee actual in-game reward rates or results.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.question1: How many rewards appear in one draw?
faq.answer1: Each draw randomly selects between 1 and 3 rewards.
faq.question2: Can the same reward appear twice in one draw?
faq.answer2: No. Every reward selected in a single draw has a unique number.
faq.question3: Does this use the same reward rates as the actual game?
faq.answer3: No. This is a simple entertainment simulator and does not reproduce the actual game's probability table.

header.title: Titan Gear Crafting Simulator
header.description: Choose a gear part and catalyst, then simulate Titan gear grades, options, and special effects using the configured probability data.

rate.title: Expected Crafting Rates
rate.notice: Expected counts are averages calculated from probability × craft count and may differ from a single simulation run.

statistics.title: Current Run Statistics
statistics.description: A summary of the generated results by grade and special effect.
statistics.empty: Run the simulator to display result statistics.

results.title: Crafting Results
results.description: Review each gear's grade, option values, and special effect.
results.empty: No results yet. Choose the crafting settings and run the simulator.

guide.introductionTitle: What Is the Titan Gear Crafting Simulator?
guide.introduction1: This tool generates possible Titan gear grades, standard options, and special effects using probability-based simulation.
guide.introduction2: The selected part, catalyst, and craft count are stored in the URL, making it easy to share or revisit the same settings.
guide.usage1: Select the Titan gear part you want to craft.
guide.usage2: Choose a catalyst or select No Catalyst.
guide.usage3: Enter a craft count between 1 and 100.
guide.usage4: Run the simulator and review the grade distribution, option values, and special effects.
guide.gradeMethod: One grade is selected from Green, Blue, Purple, and Gold using the rate table for the selected catalyst.
guide.optionMethod: Each gear receives one or two options at a 40% chance each, or three options at a 20% chance. Option values use a normal distribution so values near the midpoint occur more often.
guide.specialMethod: Special effects are rolled only for Gold gear and use the rate table assigned to the selected gear part.
guide.specialRateTitle: Special-Effect Rates for the Selected Part
guide.specialRateDescription: Conditional rates for each special effect on Gold {{part}} gear.
guide.caution1: Results are generated locally in the browser and do not guarantee actual in-game crafting outcomes.
guide.caution2: The option-value distribution uses this simulator's normal-distribution model and may differ from the game's internal algorithm.
guide.caution3: Results may become outdated if a game update changes probability tables or option ranges.
guide.caution4: Small samples can differ greatly from the configured rates; larger samples tend to approach the expected distribution.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.question1: Can the same standard option appear more than once on one gear item?
faq.answer1: Yes. The current simulator selects each option independently, so duplicate options are possible. This preserves the behavior of the original simulator.
faq.question2: Does every Gold gear item receive a special effect?
faq.answer2: No. After Gold gear is selected, it must pass a separate special-effect probability roll.
faq.question3: Are the rates exactly the same as the live game?
faq.answer3: The simulator uses the supplied probability data and option ranges, but the game's internal random and option-distribution algorithms may not be public. Treat the results as estimates.

header.title: Titan Gear Refinement Simulator
header.description: Configure Titan gear and material options, then simulate option additions, upgrades, and replacements during refinement.

settings.partDescription: Changing the part resets the gear, material, and history to that part's default values.

gear.title: Titan Gear Settings
gear.description: Configure between one and three options. Select an option to change its type and value.

material.title: Refinement Material Settings
material.description: Set the option type and value supplied by the material gear.
material.rule: One eligible option slot is selected at random. A matching option is upgraded, while a different option is replaced by the material option.

status.noEligible: No option slot is currently eligible for refinement.
status.finishEditing: Apply or cancel the current edit before refining.
status.allMax: All three matching options are already at their maximum values. Change the material option or edit the gear options.

statistics.title: Refinement Statistics
statistics.description: Summary of refinement outcomes for the current part.

history.title: Refinement History
history.added: {{option}} {{value}}% was added as a new option.

guide.introductionTitle: What Is the Titan Gear Refinement Simulator?
guide.introduction1: This tool lets you configure standard Titan gear options and a material option, then inspect how a refinement result is applied.
guide.introduction2: It uses the refinement rules from the existing code together with the option caps and increase ranges stored in the JSON data. All results are calculated locally in the browser.
guide.rule1: When the gear has an empty slot, that next empty slot can be selected as a refinement target.
guide.rule2: When the selected option matches the material option, its value increases by the configured range.
guide.rule3: When the selected option differs from the material option, it is replaced by the material option.
guide.rule4: A slot with the same option already at its maximum value is excluded from selection.
guide.notice: This is a reference simulator. It does not guarantee the live game's internal algorithm, updated values, or individual outcomes.
guide.source: Refinement increase data source: THAODIEN - s348

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.question1: Why is a maximum-value option not selected?
faq.answer1: A slot containing the same option at its maximum value cannot be upgraded further, so it is excluded from eligible targets.
faq.question2: What happens when a different option is selected?
faq.answer2: The existing option is replaced by the material gear's option type and value.
faq.question3: Are the results exactly the same as the live game?
faq.answer3: The simulator uses the registered option ranges and refinement increments, but the game's internal random algorithm and rules may change. Use it as a reference.

meta.title: Top War Eternal Land Dark Force Score Calculator | Progamer.info
meta.description: Calculate estimated hourly, daily, and final Dark Force scores in Eternal Land using the hunting area, marches, Eternal Tech, and travel time.
meta.applicationName: Top War Eternal Land Dark Force Score Calculator

hero.title: Dark Force Score Calculator
hero.description: Estimate hourly and daily scores from the hunting area, number of marches, Eternal Tech, and travel time. Enter your current score and the event end date to project your final score.

notice.title: Dark Force scores depend on the area, not the star level.
notice.description: The selected area is highlighted on the map, and its base score is used in the calculation.

map.title: Dark Force Hunting Area Map
map.imageAlt: Top War Eternal Land Dark Force hunting area map

area.label: Hunting area
area.description: The area's base Dark Force score is applied.

troop.label: Number of marches
troop.description: Total marches used for hunting at the same time.

tech.label: Eternal Tech
tech.description: Applies the Dark Force score bonus.

count.label: Rotation target count
count.description: A 240-second base rotation is included for two or more targets.

delay.label: Round-trip travel time
delay.description: Total travel time for departure and return.

cycle.title: Time per rotation
cycle.single: Based on travel time for one target

projection.title: Estimated Final Score
projection.currentScoreDescription: Your current score shown in the game.
projection.endDateDescription: Calculated to 11:00 PM on the selected date.
projection.expired: The end date has passed or is invalid.
projection.assumption: This estimate assumes continuous hunting under the current settings until the event ends.

calculation.title: How the calculation works
calculation.description: The Eternal Tech bonus is applied to the area's base score, then multiplied by the attacks per hour and the number of marches.
calculation.formula: Hourly score = area score × tech bonus × attacks per hour × marches

caution.title: Important limitations
caution.description1: Actual results may vary because of occupied targets, delayed march returns, disconnections, server conditions, or event rule changes.
caution.description2: Use this as a reference estimate that assumes uninterrupted long-term hunting.

grade.question: Does the Dark Force star level affect the score?
grade.answer: This calculator uses the base score of the hunting area rather than the Dark Force star level.

count.question: What is the rotation target count?
count.answer: For one repeated target, only travel time is used. For two or more targets, the calculator adds travel time to the 240-second base rotation.

difference.question: Why can the estimate differ from the actual score?
difference.answer: Real gameplay variables such as occupied targets, stopped marches, and network delays are not included.

meta.title: Top War Eternal Land Facility Score Map | Progamer.info
meta.description: Select occupied facilities on the Top War Eternal Land map and calculate estimated scores per minute, hour, and day.
meta.applicationName: Top War Eternal Land Facility Score Map

hero.title: Eternal Land Facility Score Map
hero.description: Select facilities on the map to calculate estimated scores earned per minute, hour, and day while they remain occupied. Select multiple facilities to compare your alliance's combined score production.

guide.title: How to use the map
guide.description: Hover over a diamond marker or focus it with the keyboard to view facility information. Select a marker to include that facility in the total.

map.title: Facility Selection Map
map.description: Glowing markers indicate currently selected facilities.
map.mobileHint: Swipe horizontally to explore the map.
map.scrollAria: Eternal Land map. On mobile devices, scroll horizontally to explore it.
map.imageAlt: Full Top War Eternal Land map showing facility locations

selectedList.title: Selected Facilities
selectedList.empty: Select facilities on the map to display their names and per-minute scores here.

calculatorLink.before: For detailed occupation-time and target-score calculations, use the
calculatorLink.label: Eternal Land Score Calculator

calculation.title: Calculation Basis
calculation.description1: Hourly scores are calculated as 60 times each facility's base per-minute score, and daily scores as 1,440 times the base score.
calculation.description2: The result is a simple estimate that assumes the selected facilities remain occupied for the entire period.

caution.title: Important Limitations
caution.description1: Actual scores may vary depending on the occupation start time, facility loss, server progress, and event-rule changes.
caution.description2: Please contact the site if the values have changed after a game update.

multiple.question: Can I select multiple facilities at once?
multiple.answer: Yes. Select facility markers one by one, and the scores of all selected facilities will be added automatically.

daily.question: Will I actually earn the full daily score shown?
daily.answer: The daily score assumes continuous occupation for 24 hours. The actual score will be lower if the occupation period is shorter or the facility is lost.

location.question: What if a facility location differs from the game map?
location.answer: Facility layouts and values may change by Eternal Land season or game update. Use the latest in-game map as the primary reference.

eternalLand.title: Eternal Land (EL)
eternalLand.description: Eight servers compete in a territorial war to determine the ultimate champion.

meta.title: Top War Eternal Land Occupation Score Calculator | Progamer.info
meta.description: Enter the Eternal Land end time, participating servers, current scores, and occupied facilities to calculate score per minute, projected final scores, and rankings.
meta.applicationName: Top War Eternal Land Occupation Score Calculator

hero.title: Eternal Land Occupation Score Calculator
hero.description: Set the end time, each server's current score, and occupied facilities to project final scores and rankings for the remaining event time. Your entries are stored in this browser for future visits.

overview.title: Project rankings in five steps
overview.description: Every step remains visible. When information is missing, the page shows an empty state and instructions so the complete calculation workflow is still clear.

deadline.title: End time
deadline.description: Set the event end date and time.

servers.title: Servers
servers.description: Add the server numbers or names to compare.

occupation.title: Occupied facilities
occupation.description: Assign occupied facilities on the map.

ranking.title: Projected ranking
ranking.description: Compare score per minute and final scores.

deadline.title: Set the End Time
deadline.description: Projected scores are calculated using the time remaining until the selected deadline.
deadline.noTime: No time remains, or the end time is invalid.

server.title: Register Servers
server.description: Add the servers whose final scores and rankings you want to compare.
server.placeholder: Enter a server number or name
server.empty: No servers have been registered. Add at least one server to begin.
server.confirmReset: Reset all registered servers and facility assignments?
server.confirmRemove: Remove server {{name}}? Its facility assignments will also be cleared.

errors.required: Enter a server number or name.
errors.duplicate: This server has already been registered.

currentScore.title: Enter Current Scores
currentScore.description: Enter each server's current accumulated score from the game.
currentScore.empty: Current-score inputs will appear after you register a server.

occupation.title: Assign Occupied Facilities · {{perspective}}
occupation.description: Select a server, then select facilities on the map to assign or remove them.
occupation.noServers: Register a server before assigning facilities.
occupation.selectServer: Select a server above to edit its occupied facilities. The map is currently in overview mode.

ranking.title: Projected Scores and Ranking
ranking.description: The calculator adds projected occupation scores for the remaining time to each server's current score.
ranking.empty: Projected scores and rankings will appear after you register servers.

calculation.title: How the calculation works
calculation.description: The per-minute scores of all facilities occupied by a server are added together, multiplied by the remaining minutes, and added to the current score.
calculation.formula: Projected final score = current score + facility score per minute × minutes remaining

usage.title: Important limitations
usage.description1: Actual results may vary because of occupation and loss times, battles between servers, or event-rule changes.
usage.description2: This tool is a reference estimate that assumes the current occupation state remains unchanged until the deadline.

conditional.question: Should later steps remain visible before they are ready?
conditional.answer: Showing every step and using empty or disabled states for unavailable inputs makes the full calculation workflow easier to understand.

finalScore.question: How is the projected final score calculated?
finalScore.answer: The calculator adds the current score to the facility score per minute multiplied by the number of minutes remaining.

storage.question: Where is my input stored?
storage.answer: Servers, facility assignments, and the end time are stored in this browser's local storage.
